leads.issues: A site search of everything Google has indexed for acetypewriter.com turns up only two pages: the homepage and a single blog post ("Keyboard Deconstructed"). There's no separately indexed About/History page telling the story of a shop that's been running continuously since 1961, and no dedicated Repair Services page describing what a manual typewriter evaluation or ink ribbon replacement actually involves — despite the homepage covering both topics. That leaves Google almost nothing specific to match against searches like "typewriter repair Portland" or "manual typewriter service" beyond one general-purpose homepage.
leads.opportunity: Splitting the homepage's existing About and Repair Services content into their own dedicated, indexable pages is a same-week fix that requires no new copywriting — just structuring what's already true about the shop. Expected within 30 days: separate pages that can each rank for their own searches ("typewriter repair Portland," "typewriter evaluation," "buy manual typewriter Portland") instead of 65 years of continuous operation competing for space on one thin homepage.
leads.email_subject: Google only has 2 pages of yours indexed
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was looking into typewriter shops around Portland and came across Ace Typewriter - 65 years in business since 1961 is a rare thing to find intact these days. I checked how the site shows up on Google though, and right now only two pages are indexed: your homepage and one blog post. There's no separate page for your history, and no dedicated page for what a repair evaluation or ribbon replacement actually involves - even though the homepage already covers both, just not in a form Google can index and rank on its own.

I put together a page showing what dedicated About and Repair Services pages would look like:
https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-17-ace-typewriter.html

You can also see how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought 65 years of keeping manual typewriters running deserved more than two pages online.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: A full Google index check for peculiarknifesharpening.com turns up only 2 pages: the homepage and an About page. There's no dedicated Pricing page, even though "how much does knife sharpening cost" is one of the first things a prospective customer searches before booking a pickup. There's also no page targeting Kansas City-specific search terms, despite the business explicitly serving the whole KC Metro on a pickup-and-delivery model.
leads.opportunity: Splitting out a Pricing page and a KC Metro service-area page from what Cliff already offers (kitchen, shop, and property tool sharpening with pickup/delivery) is a same-week fix requiring no new service to build — just structuring what's already true about the business so Google has something to match "knife sharpening pricing" and "knife sharpening Kansas City" against, instead of one general homepage doing all the work.
leads.email_subject: Your site's only showing Google 2 pages
leads.email_body:
Hi Cliff,

There's no pricing page, so anyone searching "how much does knife sharpening cost" has nothing of yours to land on, and no page built around "Kansas City" search terms either even though that's your whole service area.

I put together a page showing what a dedicated Pricing page and KC Metro service-area page would look like:

leads.issues: The homepage's indexed title tag is just "Welcome to Brown County Forge" — no service, no location, no keyword for Google to match against a search like "blacksmith classes Bloomington Indiana." Several inner pages swing the opposite direction: the Indiana University ironwork portfolio page's indexed title is "Hidden Ironwork - Indiana University Ironwork - Brown County Forge - Terran Marks the Blacksmith - Bloomington Indiana - Brown County Forge" (business name repeated twice at the end), and the knife-classes page follows the same stuffed, duplicated pattern. On top of that, the business is split across two separately indexed domains — browncountyforge.com and store.browncountyforge.com — dividing whatever search authority either one builds.
leads.opportunity: Rewriting the homepage title to lead with the actual service and location, cleaning up the stuffed/duplicated inner-page titles into single clear phrases, and mapping a plan to consolidate the store subdomain back under the main site is a fix that needs no new content — Terran already has the real proof points (1,500+ students taught since 2015, custom ironwork for Indiana University). Expected: better click-through on searches like "blacksmith classes Bloomington" and "custom house numbers Indiana" within 30-60 days, plus one domain building authority instead of two competing with each other.
leads.email_subject: Your homepage title just says 'Welcome', Terran
leads.email_body:
Hi Terran,

I was looking at Brown County Forge and searched a few things a customer might type - "blacksmith classes Bloomington Indiana," "custom house numbers Indiana" - and your homepage's title tag is just "Welcome to Brown County Forge." No service, no location, nothing for Google to match a search against.

Meanwhile some of your inner pages have the opposite problem - the Indiana University ironwork page title is stuffed with repeated keywords and ends with your business name twice in a row. And your shop is split across browncountyforge.com and store.browncountyforge.com, so any search authority you build gets divided between two domains instead of one.

None of that reflects what's actually true - 1,500+ students taught since 2015, real custom work for places like IU, a distinct hand-forged look. Google just doesn't have it yet.

Put together a quick page on what I'd fix and why: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-17-brown-county-forge.html

No pressure either way, just thought it was worth flagging since your actual track record is a lot stronger than what's showing up in search.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.industry: Bespoke Handmade Leather Goods (dog collars/leads, belts, wallets, bags) & Leathercraft Workshops
leads.issues: Several core shop pages are still indexed under their default store-builder titles — "Products - J.H. Leather" and "Collections - J.H. Leather" — with no mention of what's actually sold (handmade dog collars, leads, belts, wallets, bags). The "Be The Maker" page is indexed as "J.H.Leather - Be The Maker - J.H. Leather," repeating the business name at both ends of the title. And Jo's credential as a multi award-winning saddler doesn't appear in any of the indexed titles or the homepage's search snippet, despite being one of the strongest trust signals a bespoke leather business could show a searcher.
leads.opportunity: Rewriting the Products/Collections titles around the actual product range, fixing the duplicated "Be The Maker" title, and working the award-winning saddler credential into the homepage title and meta description is a fix that needs no new content — just structuring what's already true. Expected: better click-through on searches like "handmade leather dog collar UK" and "bespoke leather belt Wales" within 30-60 days, from a site that currently reads as a generic online shop instead of a bespoke craftsperson's workshop.
leads.email_subject: Your product pages just say 'Products', Jo
leads.email_body:
Hi Jo,

I was looking at J.H. Leather and noticed a few of your core shop pages are still using the default store-builder titles - "Products - J.H. Leather" and "Collections - J.H. Leather" - with no mention of dog collars, belts, wallets or bags. Anyone searching "handmade leather dog collar UK" has nothing specific of yours to find.

Your "Be The Maker" page title also repeats the business name twice - it's indexed as "J.H.Leather - Be The Maker - J.H. Leather." And your "multi award-winning saddler" credential, which is a genuinely rare thing to be able to say, doesn't show up anywhere in what Google displays for your site.

Put together a quick page on what I'd fix and why: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-17-jh-leather.html

No pressure either way, just thought your actual craft deserved to show up in search the way it shows up in the workshop.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder
